channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump order targets 'divisive narratives' in Smithsonian museums
item.description: President Donald Trump on Thursday signed an executive order seeking to exert more control over what is displayed at Smithsonian museums and federal sites, citing what the White House called “divisive narratives” promoted by certain museums.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KREX 5

item.title: Babysitter stabs 3-year-old girl to death in Las Vegas: police
item.description: Babysitter Marketta Phillips has been arrested for allegedly stabbing to death the 3-year-old girl she was minding in Las Vegas, police said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to know about the U.S. military's Pituffik Space Base in Greenland
item.description: NUUK, Greenland (AP) — The remote Pituffik Space Base in northwestern Greenland, which Vice President JD Vance is visiting on Friday, is the U.S. Department of Defense's northernmost installation. The base was built following a 1951 defense agreement between Denmark and the United States.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Powerful earthquake rocks Thailand and Myanmar, causing high-rise collapse
item.description: A 7.7 magnitude earthquake rocked Thailand and neighboring Myanmar midday on Friday, killing at least two people in Bangkok and burying an unknown number when a high-rise building under construction collapsed.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump administration moves to end union rights for many federal workers
item.description: The order targets agencies it says have a national security mission but many of the departments don’t have a strict national security connection.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KRON4

item.title: NFL legend Randy White fights fentanyl after personal tragedy
item.description: Jesse Dempsey, a professional snowboarder, unknowingly took a pill laced with fentanyl, believing it was a prescribed painkiller for a knee injury.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dorman, NewsNation

item.title: Turkish student at Tufts University is latest Palestinian supporter swept up in US crackdown
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A Turkish student detained by federal officers as she walked along a street in a Boston suburb is the latest supporter of Palestinian causes to be swept up in the Trump administration’s crackdown on immigrants who express their political views.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, JAKE OFFENHARTZ and KATHY McCORMACK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: First rain and then fire chase people from their homes in North and South Carolina
item.description: When Nicole Taylor and her family moved to their new home in the South Carolina mountains six months ago, the gorgeous view of Table Rock Mountain was the clincher.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS and BRITTANY PETERSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge says heat in Texas prisons unconstitutional as states face mounting lawsuits
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — A federal judge's rebuke of Texas housing prisoners in lockups without air conditioning stopped short of ordering a fix before summer in what has become of the country's biggest lawsuits over keeping prisoners safe during dangerous extreme heat. But U.S.
item.dc:creator: JIM VERTUNO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Appeals court refuses to halt an order for the rehiring of thousands of fired federal workers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An appeals court in California has refused to halt a judge’s order requiring the Trump administration to rehire thousands of federal workers who were let go in mass firings. A split 9th U.S.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST and JANIE HAR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Luigi Mangione may get a laptop in jail to work on his defense in CEO murder case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A judge cleared the way Thursday for Luigi Mangione to get a laptop behind bars — if his jailers agree — so he can examine documents and other material in the UnitedHealthcare CEO murder case.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New Jersey police officers accuse chief of turning department into 'animal house': docs
item.description: Police officers in North Bergen, New Jersey accuse their chief of creating an "animal house" where coffee pots were spiked with prescription drugs and open defecation was common.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Civil rights groups say push to dismantle Education Department will undo hard-won gains
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The rights of Americans to self-determine how to educate their children — a hotly contested matter that stretches back to at least the Civil War — have long been intertwined with the principle of equally and equitably educating children across racial lines.
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Rubio says at least 300 foreign students' visas have been revoked
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Thursday at least 300 foreign students have had their visas revoked amid the Trump administration's immigration agenda, a far higher number than was previously known.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Man arrested in Molotov cocktail attack at Tesla shop in Las Vegas will also face federal charges
item.description: Las Vegas police on Thursday said a man suspected of attacking a Tesla location with Molotov cocktails and damaging at least five vehicles has been arrested.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Here are the international students and faculty known to be targeted by ICE
item.description: The Trump administration’s crackdown on foreign students and faculty who have voiced support for the Palestinian cause has escalated, with universities across the country seeing students either arrested by Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) or having their visas revoked.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Bryan Kohberger trial: Idaho student murders survivor draws mask intruder wore on night of stabbings
item.description: An Idaho student murders survivor drew a sketch of the mask she says she saw on an intruder in her house the night four of her friends were killed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sundance Film Festival is leaving Utah for Colorado, starting in 2027
item.description: Boulder has officially been unveiled as the city to host the Sundance Film Festival starting in 2027, according to a press release from the Sundance Institute.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Kristensen, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ulster County rejects Texas motion against New York abortion prescriber
item.description: Ulster County rejected a motion seeking a civil penalty of over $100,000 against Dr. Margaret Carpenter of New Paltz over a missed court appearance for prescribing mifepristone to a Texan.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, NewsNation

item.title: Tennessee father recalls being shot while holding two infants
item.description: "I was standing there with the girls at the driver window, and he already had a gun on me," he recalled.
item.dc:creator: Kori Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Young American tourists' cause of death revealed after they were found 'motionless' at beach resort
item.description: The cause of death for three Massachusetts women who were found dead in their Belize resort hotel room last month has been revealed by city officials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Associated Press, banned from White House press pool, renews request to court for reinstatement
item.description: A lawyer for The Associated Press has asked a federal judge once again to reinstate the agency’s access to the White House press pool and some other official events.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Justice Department goes to bat for Trump in hush money case
item.description: The Justice Department went to bat for President Trump in his hush money criminal case Wednesday, urging for the case to be moved so he can attempt to toss his guilty verdict on presidential immunity grounds.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: The White House security breach raises concerns among some military spouses and veterans
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — When Alyssa Myatt’s husband served on an aircraft carrier last year, she and other U.S. Navy spouses had to follow strict security protocols that meant driving to the ship’s home port just to learn that its deployment was being extended.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY and ALLEN G. BREED, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 5 takeaways from House hearing on NPR, PBS
item.description: The heads of the nation's top public broadcasters faced an intense grilling from lawmakers Wednesday on Capitol Hill over their outlets’ funding and editorial objectivity.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KRON4

item.title: Cartel 'sicarios' crossed into US soil in murder-for-hire scheme, prosecutors say
item.description: A Mexican cartel leader has been sentenced to U.S. federal prison for a murder-for-hire scheme in which prosecutors say he brought sicarios, or hit men, across the border into Laredo, Texas.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: NTSB chairwoman says reviewing the data after midair crash may prevent the next aviation accident
item.description: Someone should have spotted the alarming number of near misses in the skies over the nation's capital before the fatal midair collision that killed 67 people in January, and reviewing the data now could prevent future crashes, according to the head of the agency investigating the crash.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Appeals court won't halt order barring Trump administration from deportations under wartime law
item.description: A federal appeals court refused Wednesday to lift an order barring the Trump administration from deporting Venezuelan migrants to El Salvador under an 18th century wartime law.
item.dc:creator: Michael Kunzelman, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Burrito lovers rejoice: Chipotle isn’t closing, despite social media rumors
item.description: Chipotle is not closing despite claims on social media. Since March 20, rumors that Chipotle was closing ran rampant on social media. Users on X. Reddit, TikTok, and elsewhere reacted to the news, with many confused about how the fast-casual eatery could be closing despite its popularity.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Ex-NFL player charged after 190 dogs seized from dog fighting venture
item.description: An Oklahoma man is facing federal charges after officials seized 190 dogs from an animal fighting venture, according to court documents.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Kermit the Frog to give commencement address at University of Maryland
item.description: Prominent political figures and lawmakers on the commencement speaker circuit are about to have some friendly, froggy competition.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, KRON4

item.title: Trump asks Supreme Court for OK to cut teacher-training money as part of anti-DEI push
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration on Wednesday asked the Supreme Court to allow it to cut hundreds of millions of dollars for teacher training.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Stepmother accused of holding Connecticut man captive for over 20 years appears in court
item.description: Kimberly Sullivan arrived at Waterbury Superior Court just before 10 a.m., where she was expected to plead not guilty to kidnapping and assault charges, according to her lawyers. But the case was continued until Friday.
item.dc:creator: Jenn Brink, Fox 5

item.title: 30 years after music icon Selena's murder, Yolanda Saldívar is up for parole. Here's what to know
item.description: Thirty years ago, music legend Selena Quintanilla-Pérez was killed by her fan club’s president, Yolanda Saldívar. For the last three decades, Saldívar has served her life sentence in Texas.
item.dc:creator: MARIA SHERMAN AP Music Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Slim chance for northern lights in some states due to 'high speed stream' from Sun
item.description: Some across the U.S. may again have a chance to see the northern lights Tuesday night as material hurled out by the sun reaches Earth.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Postmaster General Louis DeJoy resigns after 5 years in the position
item.description: The head of the beleaguered U.S. Postal Service, Louis DeJoy, resigned Monday after nearly five years in the position, leaving as President Donald Trump and Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency have floated the idea of privatizing mail service.
item.dc:creator: LISA BAUMANN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Woman killed during conjugal visit with convicted California murderer, officials say
item.description: A notorious killer serving life in prison without the possibility of parole for the murder of four men in Los Angeles County reportedly strangled his wife to death during a conjugal visit late last year, according to multiple media reports.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, WGN 9
